---
title: 'Manage the compute catalog'
---

This guide walks through common catalog workflows using both the CLI and the Python API.

**Prerequisites:**

- `git-annex` installed (`apt install git-annex` / `brew install git-annex`)
- Familiarity with xorq expressions

## Initialize a catalog

::: {.panel-tabset}

### CLI

```bash
# Named catalog (stored in ~/.local/share/xorq/annex-catalogs/my-catalog)
xorq catalog --name my-catalog init

# Catalog at a specific path
xorq catalog --path /data/catalogs/project init
```

### Python

```python
from xorq.catalog import Catalog

# By name
catalog = Catalog.from_name("my-catalog", init=True)

# By path
catalog = Catalog.from_repo_path("/data/catalogs/project", init=True)

# Default catalog
catalog = Catalog.from_default(init=True)
```

:::

## Add builds

Builds can be added from zip archives, build directories, or xorq expressions.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### CLI

```bash
# Add from a build archive
xorq catalog --name my-catalog add /path/to/build.zip

# Add with aliases
xorq catalog --name my-catalog add /path/to/build.zip --alias latest --alias v1
```

### Python

```python
from pathlib import Path
import xorq.api as xo
from xorq.catalog import Catalog

catalog = Catalog.from_name("my-catalog")

# From a zip archive
entry = catalog.add(Path("/path/to/build.zip"))

# From a build directory
entry = catalog.add(Path("/path/to/build-dir/"))

# From an expression directly
expr = xo.memtable({"x": [1, 2, 3]})
entry = catalog.add(expr, aliases=("latest", "v1"))

print(entry.name)    # content-derived hash name
print(entry.kind)    # ExprKind.Expr or ExprKind.UnboundExpr
```

:::

## List entries and aliases

::: {.panel-tabset}

### CLI

```bash
# List entries
xorq catalog --name my-catalog list

# List entries with expression kind
xorq catalog --name my-catalog list --kind

# List aliases
xorq catalog --name my-catalog list-aliases

# Show catalog metadata
xorq catalog --name my-catalog info
```

### Python

```python
catalog = Catalog.from_name("my-catalog")

# Entry names
catalog.list()                 # ['hash-abc123', 'hash-def456']

# Full entry objects
for entry in catalog.catalog_entries:
    print(entry.name, entry.kind, entry.backends)

# Aliases
catalog.list_aliases()         # ['latest', 'v1']

# Alias objects with targets
for alias in catalog.catalog_aliases:
    print(f"{alias.alias} -> {alias.catalog_entry.name}")
```

:::

## Manage aliases

Aliases are stable names that point to entries. They can be created, reassigned, and removed.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### CLI

```bash
# Create an alias
xorq catalog --name my-catalog add-alias <entry-name> production

# Remove an alias
xorq catalog --name my-catalog remove-alias production
```

### Python

```python
catalog = Catalog.from_name("my-catalog")

# Create
catalog.add_alias("hash-abc123", "production")

# Reassign (overwrites the existing symlink)
catalog.add_alias("hash-def456", "production")

# View revision history for an alias
alias = catalog.catalog_aliases[0]
for entry, commit in alias.list_revisions():
    print(f"  {commit.authored_datetime}: {entry.name} ({'exists' if entry.exists() else 'removed'})")
```

:::

## Remove entries

Removing an entry also removes any aliases pointing to it.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### CLI

```bash
xorq catalog --name my-catalog remove <entry-name>
```

### Python

```python
catalog = Catalog.from_name("my-catalog")
catalog.remove("hash-abc123")
```

:::

## Retrieve entries

Export an entry's archive to a directory, or load the expression directly.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### CLI

```bash
# Export archive to current directory
xorq catalog --name my-catalog get <entry-name>

# Export to a specific directory
xorq catalog --name my-catalog get <entry-name> --output /tmp/exports
```

### Python

```python
catalog = Catalog.from_name("my-catalog")

# Get the zip archive
zip_path = catalog.get_zip("hash-abc123", dir_path="/tmp/exports")

# Load the expression directly from an entry
entry = catalog.get_catalog_entry("hash-abc123")
expr = entry.expr  # deserialized xorq Expr
```

:::

## Remote storage

Catalogs can push artifact content to remote storage (S3, GCS, local directory) via git-annex special remotes.

### Initialize with a directory remote

```python
from xorq.catalog import Catalog
from xorq.catalog.annex import DirectoryRemoteConfig

remote_config = DirectoryRemoteConfig(
    name="myremote",
    directory="/data/annex-store",
)
catalog = Catalog.from_repo_path(
    "/path/to/catalog",
    init=True,
    remote_config=remote_config,
)
```

### Initialize with an S3 remote

```python
from xorq.catalog.annex import S3RemoteConfig

remote_config = S3RemoteConfig.make_s3_remote(
    name="s3remote",
    bucket="my-catalog-bucket",
    aws_access_key_id="...",
    aws_secret_access_key="...",
)

# Or for GCS (S3-compatible interoperability mode):
remote_config = S3RemoteConfig.make_gcs_remote(
    name="gcs",
    bucket="my-gcs-bucket",
    aws_access_key_id="...",   # GCS HMAC key
    aws_secret_access_key="...",
)

catalog = Catalog.from_repo_path("/path/to/catalog", init=True, remote_config=remote_config)
```

### Clone a remote catalog

```bash
xorq catalog clone https://github.com/org/my-catalog.git
```

```python
# For S3-backed catalogs, pass credentials at clone time:
catalog = Catalog.clone_from(
    "https://github.com/org/my-catalog.git",
    aws_access_key_id="...",
    aws_secret_access_key="...",
)
```

### Sync with remotes

::: {.panel-tabset}

### CLI

```bash
xorq catalog --name my-catalog push
xorq catalog --name my-catalog pull
xorq catalog --name my-catalog sync   # pull then push
```

### Python

```python
catalog.push()
catalog.pull()
catalog.sync()   # pull then push
```

:::

## Validate consistency

```bash
xorq catalog --name my-catalog check
```

```python
catalog.assert_consistency()  # raises AssertionError on inconsistency
```

This verifies that `catalog.yaml`, the filesystem entries, metadata files, and alias symlinks are all in agreement.

## Interactive explorer

The catalog includes a terminal UI for browsing entries and aliases:

```bash
xorq catalog --name my-catalog tui
```
